title: zapoteca formosa subsp. mollicula
intro: Zapoteca formosa subsp. mollicula is a plant species that belongs to the family Fabaceae and is commonly known as a wild legume native to Mexico. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial compounds that can help in soothing wounds and promoting healing. The plant's leaves and stems have been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, offering benefits such as reducing pain and inflammation, and supporting immune function. Herbal preparations of Zapoteca formosa subsp. mollicula can be made by infusing the leaves in hot water or using the plant's extracts in teas or tinctures.
